author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the same words of the Gospel, Matt. xxii. 42.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the same words of the Gospel, Matt. xxii. 42.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The question which was proposed to the Jews, Christians ought to solve.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord Jesus Christ, who proposed it to the Jews, did not solve it Himself, to the Jews, I mean, He did not, but to us He hath solved it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will put you in remembrance, Beloved, and ye will find that He hath solved it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But first consider the knot of the question.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He asked the Jews what they “thought of Christ, whose Son He was to be;” for they too look for the Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They read of Him in the Prophets, they expected Him to come, when He was come they killed Him; for where they read that Christ would come, there did they read that they should kill Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But His future coming they hoped for in the Prophets; for they did not see their future crime.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He therefore so questioned them about the Christ, not as if about One who was unknown to them, or whose Name they had never heard, or whose coming they had never hoped for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they err in that even yet they hope for Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And we indeed hope for Him too; but we hope for Him as One who is to come as Judge, not to be judged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Holy Prophets prophesied both, that He should come first to be judged unrighteously, that He should come afterwards to judge with righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What think ye,” then, saith he, “of Christ? whose Son is He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They answered Him, The Son of David.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this was entirely according to the Scriptures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He said, “How then doth David in spirit call Him Lord, saying, The Lord said unto My Lord, Sit Thou on My right hand, till I make Thine enemies Thy footstool.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If David then in spirit call Him Lord, how is He his Son?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here then is need of a caution, lest Christ be thought to have denied that He was the Son of David.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not deny that He was the Son of David, but He enquired the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ye have said that Christ is the Son of David, I do not deny it; but David calls Him Lord; tell me how is He his Son, who is also his Lord; tell me how?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They did not tell Him, but were silent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then tell by the explanation of Christ Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By His Apostle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But first, whereby do we prove that Christ hath Himself explained it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle says, “Would ye receive a proof of Christ who speaketh in me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then in the Apostle hath He vouchsafed to solve this question.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the first place, what said Christ speaking by the Apostle to Timothy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my Gospel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, Christ is the Son of David.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How is He also David’s Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell us, O Apostle: “who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge David’s Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou acknowledge David’s Lord, our Lord, the Lord of heaven and earth, the Lord of the Angels, equal with God, in the form of God, how is He David’s Son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark what follows.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle shows thee David’s Lord by saying, “Who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how is He David’s Son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But He emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men; and being found in fashion as a man, He humbled Himself, having become obedient unto death, even the death of the cross.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore God also hath highly exalted Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ “of the seed of David,” the Son of David, rose again because “He emptied Himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How did He “empty Himself”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By taking that which He was not, not by losing that which He was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He “emptied Himself,” He “humbled himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though He was God, He appeared as man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was despised as He walked on earth, He who made the heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was despised as though a mere man, as though of no power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, not despised only, but slain moreover.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was that stone that lay on the ground, the Jews stumbled against it, and were shaken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what doth He Himself say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be shaken, but on whomsoever it shall fall, it shall grind him to powder.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First, He lay low, and they stumbled against Him; He shall come from above, and He will “grind” them that have been shaken “to powder.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus have ye heard that Christ is both David’s Son, and David’s Lord: David’s Lord always, David’s Son in time: David’s Lord, born of the substance of His Father, David’s Son, born of the Virgin Mary, conceived by the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us hold fast both.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one of them will be our eternal habitation, the other is our deliverance from our present exile.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For unless our Lord Jesus Christ had vouchsafed to become man, man had perished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was made that which He made, that what He made might not perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Very Man, Very God; God and man whole Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the Catholic faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso denieth that Christ is God is a Photinian; whoso denieth that Christ is man is a Manichæan.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso confesseth that Christ is God equal with the Father and very man, that He truly suffered, truly shed His blood (for the Truth would not have set us free, if He had given a false price for us); whoso confesseth both, is a Catholic.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath the country, he hath the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath the country, “In the beginning was the Word;” He hath the country, “Being in the form of God, He thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath the way, “The Word was made flesh;” He hath the way, “He emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is the home whither we are going, He is the way whereby we go.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us by Him go unto Him, and we shall not go astray.
